reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe – it sources from official GitHub, uses standard VCS practices, and only copies static icon files. No build/install hooks or dangerous operations exist.
coverage_issues: qwen failed to review Git metadata files (hooks/config) – though low-risk, they're part of the source tree
Neither reviewed Numix-Circle directory (symmetry risk, but low impact)
false_positives: deepseek's review of Git metadata (exclude/packed-refs) – these aren't executed during makepkg and pose no risk
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Models should deprioritize Git internals (.git/*, hooks) unless referenced in PKGBUILD
Add coverage checks: Audit tool should flag if critical directories (like package content sources) are unreviewed
Optimize cost: qwen's focused approach (2 files) was sufficient; avoid over-reviewing inert files like .git metadata
